target.title: fix(models): resolve provider-qualified aliases in session switches
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

If this PR fixes a plugin beta-release blocker, title it `fix(<plugin-id>): beta blocker - <summary>` and link the matching `Beta blocker: <plugin-name> - <summary>` issue labeled `beta-blocker`. Contributors cannot label PRs, so the title is the PR-side signal for maintainers and automation.

  - Problem: Control UI/TUI session model switching could pass a provider-qualified display alias like `lmstudio-moe/MoE` through `sessions.patch`, which let the provider receive `MoE` instead of the canonical local model id.
  - Why it matters: LM Studio and other local providers reject display aliases as invalid model identifiers, breaking user-selected mid-session model switches.
  - What changed: Shared model selection now resolves `provider/Alias` through the configured alias index when the provider matches the alias target, before existing allowlist/catalog validation and persistence.
  - What did NOT change (scope boundary): No fallback behavior, provider-specific LM Studio logic, Telegram behavior, or broad Control UI state handling changed.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #75163
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

For bug fixes or regressions, explain why this happened, not just what changed. Otherwise write `N/A`. If the cause is unclear, write `Unknown`.

  - Root cause: `resolveModelRefFromString` resolved aliases only when the entire raw model string matched a configured alias. Bare `MoE` resolved, but `lmstudio-moe/MoE` was parsed as provider `lmstudio-moe` and model `MoE`.
  - Missing detection / guardrail: Existing tests covered bare aliases and slash-form aliases, but not provider-qualified display aliases emitted by session/model selection paths.
  - Contributing context (if known): Control UI/TUI session state can combine a server-reported model alias with its provider into `provider/Alias`, then send that value through `sessions.patch`.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

For bug fixes or regressions, name the smallest reliable test coverage that should catch this. Otherwise write `N/A`.

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
  - Target test or file: `src/agents/model-selection.test.ts`, `src/gateway/sessions- patch.test.ts`
  - Scenario the test should lock in: `lmstudio-moe/MoE` resolves and persists as `lmstudio-moe/ qwen3.6-35b-a3b` when `MoE` is configured as that model’s alias.
  - Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The resolver test locks the shared canonicalization rule; the Gateway patch test proves the user session persistence boundary stores the canonical override.
  - Existing test that already covers this (if any): Existing tests covered bare aliases and explicit provider/model refs, but not provider-qualified aliases.
  - If no new test is added, why not: N/A


## User-visible / Behavior Changes

  Users can switch to configured aliased local/provider models from Control UI/TUI without the provider receiving the display alias as the model id.

## Diagram (if applicable)

For UI changes or non-trivial logic flows, include a small ASCII diagram reviewers can scan quickly. Otherwise write `N/A`.

```text
  Before:
  [model switch: lmstudio-moe/MoE] -> [sessions.patch stores/parses MoE as model] -> [provider
  rejects invalid model id]

  After:
  [model switch: lmstudio-moe/MoE] -> [shared alias resolver maps to qwen3.6-35b-a3b] ->
  [sessions.patch persists canonical override]
```

## Security Impact (required)

  - New permissions/capabilities? (Yes/No) No
  - Secrets/tokens handling changed? (Yes/No) No
  - New/changed network calls? (Yes/No) No
  - Command/tool execution surface changed? (Yes/No) No
  - Data access scope changed? (Yes/No) No
  - If any Yes, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

  - OS: macOS local dev environment
  - Runtime/container: Node/pnpm workspace
  - Model/provider: LM Studio-style local provider alias (lmstudio-moe/MoE) covered by regression tests
  - Integration/channel (if any): Control UI/TUI session model switching via sessions.patch
  - Relevant config (redacted): agents.defaults.models["lmstudio-moe/qwen3.6-35b-a3b"].alias = "MoE"


### Steps

  1. Configure an allowed model with alias MoE for canonical ref lmstudio-moe/qwen3.6-35b-a3b.
  2. Send sessions.patch with model lmstudio-moe/MoE.
  3. Inspect the persisted session override.

### Expected

  - Session override persists provider lmstudio-moe and model qwen3.6-35b-a3b.

### Actual

  - Before this PR, lmstudio-moe/MoE was treated as direct model MoE, causing an invalid model identifier path.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

  - Verified scenarios:
      - pnpm test src/agents/model-selection.test.ts
      - pnpm test src/gateway/sessions-patch.test.ts
      - pnpm test src/agents/model-selection.test.ts src/gateway/sessions-patch.test.ts ui/src/ui/
        chat-model-ref.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat-model-select-state.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/
        chat.test.ts
      - pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/model-selection-shared.ts src/agents/model-selection.test.ts src/gateway/sessions-patch.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat-model-ref.ts ui/src/ui/chat-model-ref.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat-model-select-state.test.ts
      - pnpm build
      - git diff --check
      - pnpm dlx codex review --base origin/main
  - Edge cases checked: Existing slash-form alias coverage still passes; explicit allowlisted provider/model refs still pass; provider-qualified alias only resolves when the provider matches the alias target.
  - What you did not verify: Live LM Studio server behavior; Testbox pnpm check:changed was blocked because Blacksmith CLI was not authenticated and no BLACKSMITH_ORG was configured.

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

  - Backward compatible? (Yes/No) Yes
  - Config/env changes? (Yes/No) No
  - Migration needed? (Yes/No) No
  - If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

List only real risks for this PR. Add/remove entries as needed. If none, write `None`.

  - Risk: A provider/Alias string could be mistaken for a real provider/model ref.
      - Mitigation: The new resolution only applies when the suffix matches a configured alias and the prefix provider matches the alias target provider; otherwise existing parsing and allowlist validation are preserved.

### Built with Codex

target.title: fix(session): persist memory on auto daily/idle rollover
target.body:
## Summary

Auto-rollover (daily or idle stale-detection) takes a different code path than /reset and /new commands, so the bundled session-memory hook never sees a command:reset event and pre-reset transcripts go unwritten. Low-activity agents that get rolled over at 4am without ever compacting or typing a reset command silently lose their daily memory entry.

This PR fires the same internal command:reset hook on stale rollover with a commandSource of 'auto:daily' or 'auto:idle' so existing listeners (including the bundled session-memory handler) persist memory for automatic reset paths too. Guarded against firing on explicit /reset (handled by emitResetCommandHooks) and on fresh sessions.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Skills / tool execution

- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related #8185, #37955
- Closes #11960
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Human verification

Verified that the hook fires and creates the memory file on automatic reset.

target.title: fix(gateway): prefer current route delivery context
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes gateway agent delivery resolution so a concrete current request route (`channel`, `to`, `accountId`, `threadId`) wins over stale persisted session `deliveryContext` fields when the channels conflict.
- Clears stale route fields on channel switches when the new request does not provide replacement `to`/`accountId`/`threadId` values, avoiding mixed-channel routes.
- Preserves existing session-route behavior for normal follow-ups, `channel=last`, and same-channel existing-session routes.
- Compares same-channel targets through the channel parser so equivalent encodings do not clear stored threads.
- Populates `runContext.currentChannelId` from the resolved delivery target so downstream message-tool fallback inference sees the active route.
- Adds regression tests for the #68378 shape, channel-only stale-route clearing, same-channel partial updates, and canonical equivalent targets.

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #68378
- Related prior art: #22205 identified the missing `resolvedTo` -> `runContext.currentChannelId` propagation, but that PR is closed unmerged and does not address stale delivery-context precedence.
- Related but not duplicates: #45514, #65847, #33619, #39557, #51812, #52204 all describe adjacent multi-channel/stale-routing problems or other delivery paths; none is an open satisfactory PR for this gateway agent route-precedence bug.

## Root Cause / Diagnosis

`src/gateway/server-methods/agent.ts` built `requestDeliveryHint` from the active gateway request, but then merged it with the stored session `deliveryContext` in a way that made persisted fields authoritative. That let stale persisted fields, such as Feishu `channel`/`to`, override a current Discord request before the agent command and session update were assembled. The same path also did not pass the resolved target into `runContext.currentChannelId`, so tool fallback routing could miss the current route even when `resolvedTo` was known.

A second edge case appears when the request changes only `channel`: route-field fallback must not pair a new channel with stale `lastTo`/`lastAccountId`/`lastThreadId` from the old channel.

A third edge case appears when the request and session refer to the same target with different channel-owned encodings, such as Slack `C1` and `channel:C1`: raw string comparison can falsely treat that as a target change and clear the stored thread.

## Fix

- Detect when a concrete request channel conflicts with the stored session delivery channel.
- In that cross-channel conflict case, let the current request delivery hint win and use persisted delivery metadata only as fallback for fields provided by the current route.
- Clear stale last-route fields when the current channel conflicts and no replacement field is available.
- For same-channel route conflicts, preserve unchanged target/account/thread fields unless the concrete target itself changes.
- Compare request/session targets using the channel parser before treating `to` as a target change, preserving stored threads for equivalent target spellings.
- For non-conflict cases, keep existing session delivery precedence so `channel=last`, normal follow-ups, and existing subagent routes behave as before.
- Pass `resolvedTo` into `runContext.currentChannelId` alongside the existing channel/account/thread context.
- Lock the behavior with regression tests that start from stale Feishu metadata, assert Discord wins or stale route fields are cleared, preserve partial same-channel updates, and keep Slack threads when `C1` and `channel:C1` are equivalent.

## Duplicate Sweep

Searched GitHub issues/PRs for combinations of `#68378`, Discord/Feishu wrong-channel delivery, stale `deliveryContext`, gateway agent delivery, `currentChannelId`, and multi-channel session routing. Closest results:

- #22205: overlapping `currentChannelId` propagation only; closed unmerged; no stale context precedence fix or regression for #68378.
- #35451: broader `chat.send`/cron canonical route derivation; closed unmerged; different call sites.
- #67508: target-agent bound account propagation for Matrix subagent spawns; adjacent account routing, different bug.
- #45514, #65847, #33619, #39557, #51812, #52204: related issue reports/bug class, not acceptable duplicate PRs for this exact gateway agent request-route overwrite.

Conclusion: no duplicate or already-satisfactory PR found for #68378.

## Attribution

- Thanks @Suidge for the #68378 report and follow-up logs that isolated Discord input being delivered through Feishu outbound.
- Thanks @Galygious for prior analysis in #22205 around forwarding `resolvedTo` into `runContext.currentChannelId`; this PR incorporates that same missing-context piece and adds the route-precedence fix needed for #68378.

## Testing

Fresh `origin/main` base: `64089fd15ed4ba8296612b1a622d11b5662c8b96`

- `pnpm test -- src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts -t "uses the current request route"`
- `pnpm test -- src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts`
- `pnpm test -- src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts -t "preserves the stored thread when the request target is canonically equivalent"`
- `pnpm test -- src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts src/gateway/server.agent.gateway-server-agent-a.test.ts src/gateway/server.agent.gateway-server-agent-b.test.ts src/gateway/server.agent.subagent-delivery-context.test.ts`

The final command covers the CI shard failures observed after the first draft and now passes locally with 71 tests.

Scoped lint note: `pnpm lint -- src/gateway/server-methods/agent.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts` is currently blocked before linting these files by the existing `src/agents/skills/*` plugin SDK boundary type errors already present on `origin/main`.

## Review Follow-up

- Addressed and resolved Codex review thread about clearing stale route fields on channel-switch merges in `5825aede6c7568f8cec2af2653d4851bf3296d78`.
- Addressed and resolved Codex review thread about preserving unchanged same-channel route fields in `d8f4d09a343f30d82df2bdeea9541a707923a72d`.
- Addressed and resolved Codex review thread about canonical target comparison in `af7506771a0fd734024ecc2b51b23b7945f27b21`.

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No

The change narrows routing behavior to prefer the active request route only when it conflicts with stale persisted delivery metadata, and preserves stored thread context for equivalent same-channel target spellings.

target.title: fix: normalize visible assistant output before delivery
target.body:
## Summary
Gemma was sometimes leaking hidden internal text into Discord replies.
This PR makes OpenClaw clean the final reply in one shared place before sending it.
It adds regression tests for the real bad cases we saw, including leaked `<channel|>` markers and runaway repeated replies.
The fix is shared, not Gemma-only, so every delivery path uses the same cleanup.


## What changed
- teach the shared visible-text normalizer to:
  - strip leaked `<channel|>` control delimiters when they behave like leaked scaffolding
  - preserve literal `<channel|>` mentions in ordinary prose
  - preserve explicitly requested quoted targets, including full repeated strings and visible outputs that end with `<channel|>`
  - honor explicit repeat-count requests like `repeat "ABCD-1234" exactly twice`
  - drop truncated trailing leaked delimiters after long internal preambles
  - keep real leaks visible even when the preamble previously mentioned `<channel|>` literally
  - prefer the minimal repeated unit when a no-delimiter runaway answer repeats a mistakenly doubled exact target many times
  - preserve meaningful leading indentation in delivery mode while still trimming incidental single-space padding
- apply delivery-mode sanitization in the final delivery paths, while keeping stream-update sanitization lighter
- defer chunked `text_end` delivery until the final flush so partial raw scaffold chunks are never emitted early
- mark chunked `message_end` drains as final delivery so the delivery sanitizer runs on the last emitted chunks
- preserve plain streamed chunks without directive parsing when no inline directives are present
- preserve legacy `text_end` reply directives and keep intermediate chunk drains in history mode until final delivery
- reset `blockState` when `text_end` replaces the block buffer with already-sanitized final text, so stale reasoning-tag state cannot strip the final answer on flush

## Regression tests added first
- long prose that literally mentions `<channel|>` stays intact
- `plan: <channel|>visible` is treated as leaked scaffolding
- explicitly requested repeated outputs stay intact when named literally, in quotes, or via repeat-count instructions
- explicitly requested visible outputs that end with `<channel|>` stay intact
- literal marker explanations after an initial leaked delimiter stay intact
- truncated leaked delimiters after long preambles are dropped
- leaked delimiters are still stripped when earlier preamble text mentioned `<channel|>` literally
- repeated exact-target salvage prefers the minimal unit when a no-delimiter runaway answer duplicates the target in the model preamble and then repeats it many times
- legacy `text_end` reply directives still work
- chunked `message_end` drains are marked as final delivery
- intermediate chunk drains keep indentation before `text_end`
- delivery-mode sanitization keeps meaningful leading indentation while still trimming incidental padding
- chunk-boundary reasoning tags do not eat the final flushed answer

## Verification
- failing regressions were added first and reproduced locally before the fixes
- focused tests:
  - `pnpm exec vitest run src/shared/text/repeated-visible-suffix.test.ts src/shared/text/assistant-visible-text.test.ts`
  - `pnpm exec vitest run src/shared/text/repeated-visible-suffix.test.ts src/shared/text/assistant-visible-text.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-utils.strip-model-special-tokens.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.messages.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.reply-tags.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-pi-session.emits-block-replies-text-end-does-not.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-pi-session.keeps-indented-fenced-blocks-intact.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/reply-utils.test.ts`
- `pnpm build`
- live Discord probe on `vllm/gemma4-e4b` after restarting the local gateway on this branch:
  - Looper prompt: `reply with exactly pr68986-live2-1776657698 and nothing else`
  - Bob visible Discord reply: `pr68986-live2-1776657698`

target.title: feat(googlechat): add sessionThread option to bind OpenClaw sessions to Google Chat threads
target.body: AI-assisted: yes (Claude Opus 4.7). ## Summary - Problem: In Google Chat, all messages in a space shared a single OpenClaw session. Starting a new thread to change topic still inherited memory from prior threads, and replies did not reliably land in the user's inbound thread. - Why it matters: Chat threads are the natural topic boundary in Google Chat. Leaking context across threads produces confusing replies ("why does the bot remember something from a different thread?"), forces multiple concurrent threads to queue sequentially on one session, and breaks the mental model users already have from Slack. Requested in #13373. - What changed: Added an opt-in `sessionThread` channel option. When `true`, the OpenClaw session peer id is derived from the inbound thread name, so each Google Chat thread is its own conversation. Replies are now threaded via `thread.name` + `messageReplyOption=REPLY_MESSAGE_FALLBACK_TO_NEW_THREAD`, so bot responses land in the user's thread. - What did NOT change (scope boundary): Default behavior is unchanged (`sessionThread: false` keeps space-scoped sessions). No changes to auth, allowlists, webhook routing, DM flow, reactions, or attachments. No slash commands / `/focus`-style bindings added (see "future work" below). ## Change Type (select all) - [x] Bug fix - [x] Feature - [ ] Refactor required for the fix - [x] Docs - [ ] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [ ] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [x] Integrations - [ ] API / contracts - [ ] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Closes #13373 (per-thread session mode feature request) - Closes #41788 (Google Chat replies creating new threads instead of staying in the existing one) - Related #44347 — resolves the first of the two requests in that issue - [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression ### Note on naming vs the issue's proposal The issue proposes `threadSessionMode: "per-thread" | "per-space"`. This PR ships the same behavior under a boolean flag `sessionThread: boolean` (default `false` = per-space, `true` = per-thread). Rationale: - A boolean matches the two-state nature of the option and is simpler to document. - The name aligns with the existing session-scoping vocabulary in the codebase. - Happy to rename to `threadSessionMode` with a string enum if maintainers prefer the issue's exact wording, it's a straightforward rename. ## Root Cause (if applicable) - Root cause: The Google Chat channel used the space id as the session peer id and did not pass the inbound thread name back on outbound messages. That coupling meant (a) all threads in a space mapped to one session, and (b) replies could fall back to a new thread instead of the user's. - Missing detection / guardrail: No peer-id unit coverage for Google Chat; Slack already has `prepare.thread-session-key.test.ts` as prior art for this exact pattern, but nothing equivalent existed for Google Chat. - Contributing context (if known): Google Chat threads were less prominent historically in OpenClaw flows; the space-scoped default was acceptable until users started relying on threads to separate topics. ## Regression Test Plan (if applicable) - Coverage level that should have caught this: - [x] Unit test - [ ] Seam / integration test - [ ] End-to-end test - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient - Target test or file: `extensions/googlechat/src/monitor.session-thread.test.ts` - Scenario the test should lock in: `resolveGoogleChatPeerId` returns the thread name when `sessionThread` is enabled and an inbound thread is present; falls back to `spaceId` otherwise; and produces distinct peer ids for distinct threads so sessions do not cross. - Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The peer id is the single point where "which session does this message belong to?" is decided for Google Chat. Locking its behavior at the helper boundary catches the regression without pulling in the full monitor stack. - Existing test that already covers this (if any): None for Google Chat. Slack's `prepare.thread-session-key.test.ts` covers the analogous primitive. ## User-visible / Behavior Changes - New opt-in config: `channels.googlechat.sessionThread` (default `false`). - `false` (default): space-scoped sessions, prior behavior unchanged. - `true`: one OpenClaw session per Google Chat thread; a new thread starts a fresh conversation. - Outbound messages now consistently target the inbound thread via `thread.name` + `messageReplyOption=REPLY_MESSAGE_FALLBACK_TO_NEW_THREAD`, so replies land in the user's thread more reliably. This applies regardless of `sessionThread`. ## Diagram (if applicable) ```text sessionThread: false (default, unchanged) space S, thread A -> session(peer=S) space S, thread B -> session(peer=S) # shared memory sessionThread: true space S, thread A -> session(peer=A) space S, thread B -> session(peer=B) # isolated memory space S, no thread -> session(peer=S) # falls back to space-scoped ``` ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? No - Secrets/tokens handling changed? No - New/changed network calls? No (same Chat API endpoints; only request payload now sets `thread.name` and `messageReplyOption`) - Command/tool execution surface changed? No - Data access scope changed? No (in fact, `sessionThread: true` narrows memory scope per thread) ## Repro + Verification ### Environment - OS: macOS - Runtime/container: Node 22 / local gateway (`pnpm openclaw gateway restart`) - Model/provider: any (provider-agnostic) - Integration/channel (if any): Google Chat - Relevant config (redacted): ```jsonc { "channels": { "googlechat": { "enabled": true, "serviceAccountFile": "/path/to/service-account.json", "sessionThread": true } } } ``` ### Steps 1. In a Google Chat space, mention the bot in thread A and ask an identifying question (e.g. "remember the color red"). 2. Start a new thread B in the same space and ask "what color did I mention?". 3. Reply again inside thread A and ask the same question. ### Expected - `sessionThread: true`: in thread B, the bot has no memory of thread A. In thread A, the bot still remembers "red". Replies land in the thread the user messaged in. - `sessionThread: false` (default): behavior is unchanged from prior releases. ### Actual - Matches expected for both modes. ## Evidence - [x] Failing test/log before + passing after ``` pnpm test extensions/googlechat/src/monitor.session-thread.test.ts ✓ resolveGoogleChatPeerId returns spaceId when sessionThread is disabled ✓ resolveGoogleChatPeerId returns spaceId when sessionThread is undefined (default) ✓ resolveGoogleChatPeerId returns thread name when sessionThread is enabled and inbound has a thread ✓ resolveGoogleChatPeerId falls back to spaceId when sessionThread is enabled but inbound has no thread ✓ resolveGoogleChatPeerId produces distinct peer ids for distinct threads so sessions don't cross Test Files 1 passed (1) Tests 5 passed (5) ``` - [ ] Trace/log snippets - [x] Screenshot/recording - When disabled(previous behavior): - When enabled: - [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant) ## Human Verification (required) - Verified scenarios: - Manually verified in a real Google Chat space against a local gateway: - Bot replies land inside the user's inbound thread (both existing threads and thread-starter messages). - With `sessionThread: true`: new thread = fresh session; prior thread keeps its own context; no cross-thread memory leakage. - With `sessionThread: false`: unchanged, space-scoped memory. - Edge cases checked: - Inbound message with no thread (thread-starter): falls back to space-scoped session as designed. - Two concurrent threads in the same space maintain separate sessions. - What you did **not** verify: - Bot-initiated outbound into a space with no prior inbound thread context (no webhook-origin thread). This PR intentionally does not introduce a `threadKey` fallback for that case; the follow-up work below can add richer binding if demand emerges. - DM flow (no behavior change expected; DM scope is already per-channel-peer). ## Review Conversations - [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR. - [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment. ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? Yes - Config/env changes? Yes, additive only: new optional `channels.googlechat.sessionThread` boolean (default `false`). - Migration needed? No. Existing configs continue to behave as before. - If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A. To opt in, set `channels.googlechat.sessionThread: true`. ## Risks and Mitigations - Risk: Users relying on cross-thread memory in a space would lose that if they set `sessionThread: true` without realizing the implication. - Mitigation: Default is `false`; docs call out the per-thread isolation explicitly; the name `sessionThread` signals the intent. - Risk: `resolveGoogleChatPeerId` is a new exported helper surface from the plugin's `monitor.ts`. - Mitigation: Kept it narrow (one pure function) and it is only consumed inside the extension itself; no public SDK subpath added. ## Future Work (non-blocking) This PR adds the minimal primitive, matching Slack's auto thread-scoped session behavior, just gated behind an opt-in flag for Google Chat. A follow-up could align with the Telegram/Discord `session.threadBindings` model (`/focus`, `/unfocus`, `/session idle`, `/session max-age`) on top of this primitive. That work requires registering slash commands in the Chat API app config and wiring `SLASH_COMMAND` events, which is out of scope here.

target.title: fix: #77909 handle interactive card in feishu merge_forward sub-message
target.body:
## Summary

When a Feishu (飞书) message containing interactive cards (e.g., tables, forms) is merged and forwarded to the bot, the merge_forward sub-message content is not parsed correctly. The `formatSubMessageContent` function in the merge_forward handling pipeline does not have a case for `interactive` content type, causing it to fall through to the default branch which returns the placeholder `[interactive]`. This means card/table content is completely lost before the agent can read it.

The fix reuses the existing `parseInteractiveCardContent` function (already used by the regular Feishu message parsing path in `send.ts`) to handle `interactive` sub-messages within merge_forward content. The function extracts card element text (titles, table cells, button labels) from the Feishu interactive card structure.

Fixes #77909

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: Feishu merge_forward messages with interactive card content (tables, forms, etc.) now have their text content extracted instead of being replaced with a `[interactive]` placeholder.

**Real environment tested**: N/A (source-level verification — see evidence below)

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: See below
```bash
# TypeScript type check
npx tsc --noEmit --pretty extensions/feishu/src/bot-content.ts

# Run all feishu bot tests (including merge_forward tests)
npx vitest run extensions/feishu/src/bot.test.ts
```

**After-fix evidence**: See below
```
TypeScript: No errors found

✓ extensions/feishu/src/bot.test.ts (87 tests) PASS (87) FAIL (0)
```
All 87 existing tests pass. The fix is a 6-line addition that adds an `interactive` case to the `formatSubMessageContent` switch, reusing the existing `parseInteractiveCardContent` exported from `send.ts`.

**Observed result after the fix**: `interactive` sub-messages within merge_forward content are now parsed through `parseInteractiveCardContent`, which extracts card element text (title, body, table cells) instead of returning `[interactive]`.

**What was not tested**: Live end-to-end test with a real Feishu tenant (requires a running Feishu bot with WebSocket credentials, which is not available in this environment). The fix is verified at the source level: the existing `parseInteractiveCardContent` function handles the same `msg_type: "interactive"` structure that merge_forward sub-messages carry, and the new code path mirrors exactly how `parseFeishuMessageContent` handles interactive messages.

## Tests and validation

### Unit tests

All 87 existing tests pass:
```
✓ extensions/feishu/src/bot.test.ts (87 tests) PASS (87) FAIL (0)
```

### Integration / E2E

Not tested — requires a live Feishu tenant with bot configured. The fix is entirely internal to the message parsing pipeline and is covered by existing test infrastructure.

## Risk checklist

- [x] This change is backwards compatible
- [x] This change has been tested with existing configurations
- [ ] I have updated relevant documentation
- [ ] Breaking changes (if any) are documented in Summary

## Current review state

- [x] Self-review completed
- [ ] At least one maintainer review
- [ ] All CI checks passed

target.title: fix(whatsapp): reuse active QR and preserve runtime warnings
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

- Problem: even after the QR preflight contract, WhatsApp still had two remaining coherence gaps: the gateway could ignore an already-active QR until after preflight, and runtime warnings could disappear when linked state was temporarily unknown.
- Why it matters: users could get an unnecessary unstable-auth result instead of reusing a valid QR, and operational status could lose important reconnect/logged-out signals during auth-stabilizing windows.
- What changed: this PR adds an optional existing-QR hook ahead of preflight, uses it in WhatsApp, and restores runtime/session warnings when linked state is unknown.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this does not broaden QR behavior to other channels beyond the new optional hook; only WhatsApp implements it here.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [x] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #67815
- Related #67818
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: the gateway QR flow still had no pre-preflight “existing result” seam, and WhatsApp runtime warning collection still over-relied on a known linked bit.
- Missing detection / guardrail: no optional `loginWithQrStartExisting` hook in the shared gateway adapter, and no focused regression coverage for runtime warnings under unknown linked state.
- Contributing context (if known): this is the last QR/runtime coherence follow-up after #67815 and the QR preflight PR.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `extensions/whatsapp/src/login-qr.test.ts`
  - `extensions/whatsapp/src/status-issues.test.ts`
  - `src/gateway/server-methods/web.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: gateway login returns an existing QR before preflight rejection, and runtime/session warnings still surface when linked state is unknown.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the remaining behavior is isolated to the WhatsApp QR/runtime warning seam and the shared gateway login ordering.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): #67818 covers the QR preflight/result-code layer.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- `web.login.start` can reuse an already-active WhatsApp QR before preflight rejects the attempt.
- WhatsApp runtime/session warnings still show up when linked state is temporarily unknown.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[existing QR] -> [gateway preflight timeout] -> [unstable result]
[linked unknown] -> [status collector] -> [missing runtime warning]

After:
[existing QR] -> [existing-result hook] -> [reuse QR]
[linked unknown] -> [status collector] -> [runtime/session warning still visible]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (No)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (No)
- New/changed network calls? (No)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (No)
- Data access scope changed? (No)
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux
- Runtime/container: Node 22 / pnpm workspace
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): WhatsApp QR login and status surfaces
- Relevant config (redacted): default WhatsApp account config

### Steps

1. Keep an existing WhatsApp QR active, then trigger `web.login.start` during an auth-stabilizing window.
2. Exercise WhatsApp status collection while linked state is unknown but runtime/session state is still present.
3. Verify the existing QR is reused first and runtime warnings still surface.

### Expected

- Existing QR is returned before preflight rejection.
- Runtime/session warnings remain visible when linked state is unknown.

### Actual

- Verified with focused WhatsApp login/status and gateway coverage after the change.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios:

target.title: fix(gateway.tls): reject empty/whitespace certPath and keyPath
target.body:
## Summary

`gateway.tls.certPath` and `gateway.tls.keyPath` both accept `""` and whitespace-only strings at the schema layer (`z.string().optional()` with no `.min(1)`), and the runtime fallback `cfg.certPath ?? path.join(baseDir, "...")` only triggers on `null`/`undefined`, so empty strings reach `generateSelfSignedCert` unchanged. From there `path.dirname("") === "."` and `execFileAsync(openssl, [..., "-out", "", "-keyout", ""])` produces a cryptic openssl error that does not point at the real configuration mistake.

Sibling field `caPath` already guards against this via `cfg.caPath ? resolveUserPath(cfg.caPath) : undefined` (truthy check), so the fix brings `certPath`/`keyPath` to the same defensive style.

Three changes:

1. **Schema** (`src/config/zod-schema.ts:1108-1109`): Tighten to `z.string().trim().min(1).optional()` so the schema rejects `""` and whitespace-only paths at validation time.
2. **Runtime** (`src/infra/tls/gateway.ts:83-84`): Replace `??` with explicit `typeof === "string" && trim()` fallback so external callers that bypass the schema (tests, programmatic config) still get the default path. Aligns with the existing `caPath` pattern on line 85.
3. **chmod error visibility** (`src/infra/tls/gateway.ts:65-66`): Rethrow the chmod error instead of `.catch(() => {})` so any future path-related chmod failure surfaces with the offending path in the error message.

## Linked context

N/A (no existing issue)

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** `gateway.tls.certPath=""` passed schema validation and the `??` fallback did not trigger for empty strings, so `""` reached `generateSelfSignedCert` and produced a cryptic openssl error.
- **Real environment tested:** Node 22.19, Linux x86_64, local checkout of `openclaw/openclaw` main (5372c7146b).
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  ```bash
  node --import tsx /tmp/verify-tls-empty-path.mjs
  ```
  Script imports `loadGatewayTlsRuntime` from the patched source and verifies empty-string fallback, whitespace fallback, and the disabled-config regression guard.
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal capture of `node` runtime verification (copied live output):

  ```
  === gateway.tls empty path runtime verification ===

  === Test 1: empty certPath/keyPath ===

    Input: certPath="", keyPath=""
    certPath resolved: /home/.../.openclaw/gateway/tls/gateway-cert.pem
    keyPath resolved:  /home/.../.openclaw/gateway/tls/gateway-key.pem
    certPath valid: PASS
    keyPath valid:  PASS

  === Test 2: whitespace-only paths ===

    Input: certPath="   ", keyPath="\t"
    certPath resolved: /home/.../.openclaw/gateway/tls/gateway-cert.pem
    keyPath resolved:  /home/.../.openclaw/gateway/tls/gateway-key.pem
    certPath valid: PASS
    keyPath valid:  PASS

  === Test 3: valid paths (regression guard) ===

    Input: enabled: false (no certPath/keyPath)
    enabled: false (expected: false)
    required: false (expected: false)
    PASS

  === ALL PASSED ===
  ```

- **Observed result after fix:** Empty and whitespace-only `certPath`/`keyPath` now fall back to the default gateway TLS directory (`~/.openclaw/gateway/tls/gateway-cert.pem`) instead of passing `""` to openssl. The disabled-config path continues to work unchanged.
- **What was not tested:** End-to-end openssl execution with a real certificate generation (covered by the existing `loadGatewayTlsRuntime` tests which verify the full TLS load path).

## Tests and validation

```
$ pnpm test src/config/zod-schema.tls.test.ts src/infra/tls/gateway.test.ts
✓ Test Files  2 passed (2)
✓ Tests      10 passed (10)
```

## Risk checklist

- [x] Does this change affect user-visible behavior? **Yes** — users who previously had empty or whitespace-only `certPath`/`keyPath` will now get the default gateway TLS path instead of a cryptic openssl error.
- [x] Does this change affect configuration? **Yes** — empty/whitespace strings for `certPath`/`keyPath` are now rejected by schema validation (`trim().min(1)`). This is backwards-compatible: empty paths previously produced obscure openssl failures; they now produce a clear validation error.
- [x] Does this change affect security? **No**
- [x] Does this change affect plugins or providers? **No**
- [x] Does this change affect docs? **No**
- [x] Is this change backwards-compatible? **Yes** — all previously-valid non-empty paths continue to work identically. Empty/whitespace paths that previously silently broke now either fail validation or fall back to default.

target.title: fix(build): add missing config/sessions/store.runtime entry
target.body:
## Problem

Runtime error when session-override loads the session store:

```
Error [ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND]: Cannot find module '/path/to/dist/store.runtime-eZQ7KuiZ.js'
```

## When This Bug Is Triggered

This bug is triggered when users configure **multiple auth profiles** (e.g., rotating between OpenAI and Anthropic providers). Specifically, it occurs in two code paths:

1. **Manual auth profile override** — When a user explicitly selects a specific provider auth profile via session override
2. **Automatic auth profile rotation** — When the system auto-rotates providers based on cooldown policy

In both cases, the system persists the session auth profile override state to disk via `updateSessionStore()`, which triggers the dynamic import of `store.runtime.js`.

Users with a single provider (the default setup) will not hit this bug.

## Root Cause

`src/agents/auth-profiles/session-override.ts` dynamically imports `../../config/sessions/store.runtime.js` via `import()`, which tsdown cannot statically analyze. The entry was missing from `buildCoreDistEntries()`, so the file was not emitted as a standalone chunk.

Introduced in `c0933e2fc8` (2026-03-22, `perf(reply): lazy-load session store writes`).

## Fix

Added a namespaced entry to `buildCoreDistEntries()` in `tsdown.config.ts`:

```ts
"config/sessions/store.runtime": "src/config/sessions/store.runtime.ts"
```

This emits `dist/config/sessions/store.runtime.js` at the path where `session-override.js` expects to find it via relative import (`"./config/sessions/store.runtime.js"`).

## Testing

- `npm run build` emits `dist/config/sessions/store.runtime.js` ✅
- Regression test added in `src/infra/tsdown-config.test.ts` ✅
- Gateway restart confirms error is resolved ✅

Same pattern as #65735 (missing tsdown entries for dynamically-imported runtime modules).

target.title: fix(tools): surface empty availability group diagnostics at descriptor registration
target.body:
> **AI-assisted PR.** Implemented and verified with Claude (Claude Code). I have reviewed and understand the change and own the final diff.

## Summary

- Empty `allOf`/`anyOf` availability groups silently hid tools from the plan with no observable signal. Plugin authors had no way to know their descriptor was malformed.
- Root cause: `capturePluginToolDescriptor` did not preserve tool-authored `availability` expressions, and no validation ran at registration time.
- Fix: preserve `availability` on captured descriptors and surface `unsupported-signal` diagnostics via `console.warn` once at descriptor-registration time (not in the planner hot path).

Fixes #92361

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

**Behavior addressed**:
Malformed tool descriptors with empty availability groups now produce a visible `console.warn` at descriptor-registration time instead of silently hiding the tool with no signal.

**Real environment tested**:
Linux 4.19.112-2.el8.x86_64, Node.js v24.13.1, OpenClaw PR branch @ d8907360, tsx for runtime probing.

**Before-fix evidence**:
```text
# Empty anyOf → tool silently hidden, no warning, no log
node --import tsx --no-warnings -e "
import { capturePluginToolDescriptor } from './src/plugins/tool-descriptor-cache.js';
capturePluginToolDescriptor({
  pluginId: 'proof-plugin',
  tool: { name: 'proof_tool', description: 'test', parameters: { type: 'object' }, availability: { anyOf: [] } } as any,
  optional: false,
});
console.log('No warning was emitted — tool silently captured with broken availability');
"
# Output: No warning was emitted — tool silently captured with broken availability
```

The tool descriptor is captured and later hidden by `buildToolPlan` with zero observable signal. Plugin authors have no way to diagnose why their tool never appears in the plan.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```bash
node --import tsx --no-warnings -e "
import { capturePluginToolDescriptor } from './src/plugins/tool-descriptor-cache.js';

// Test 1: empty anyOf
capturePluginToolDescriptor({
  pluginId: 'proof-plugin',
  tool: { name: 'proof_tool', description: 'test', parameters: { type: 'object' }, availability: { anyOf: [] } } as any,
  optional: false,
});

// Test 2: empty allOf
capturePluginToolDescriptor({
  pluginId: 'proof-plugin',
  tool: { name: 'allof_tool', description: 'test', parameters: { type: 'object' }, availability: { allOf: [] } } as any,
  optional: false,
});

// Test 3: valid expression (no warning expected)
capturePluginToolDescriptor({
  pluginId: 'proof-plugin',
  tool: { name: 'valid_tool', description: 'test', parameters: { type: 'object' }, availability: { kind: 'always' } } as any,
  optional: false,
});
"
```

**After-fix evidence**:
```text
[plugins] tool descriptor authoring error (proof-plugin/proof_tool): Empty availability anyOf group
[plugins] tool descriptor authoring error (proof-plugin/allof_tool): Empty availability allOf group
```

- Empty `anyOf` → `console.warn` emitted with pluginId + tool name + diagnostic message ✅
- Empty `allOf` → `console.warn` emitted with pluginId + tool name + diagnostic message ✅
- Valid `kind: "always"` → no warning (correctly silent) ✅
- No availability → no warning (backward compatible) ✅

**Observed result after the fix**:
Descriptor authors immediately see which tool has a malformed availability expression at registration time (once per captured descriptor — descriptors are cached). The evaluator and planner stay pure.

**What was not tested**:
Live gateway startup with a real external plugin that has malformed availability metadata. The descriptor capture path operates on in-memory descriptors and has no platform-specific logic.

## Tests and validation

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/tool-descriptor-cache.test.ts
# Test Files  1 passed (1)
# Tests       10 passed (10)

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/tools.optional.test.ts
# Test Files  1 passed (1)
# Tests       67 passed (67)

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/tools/planner.test.ts
# Test Files  1 passed (1)
# Tests       6 passed (6)

node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental false
# passed
```

New test cases:
1. warns when tool has empty availability `anyOf` group
2. warns when tool has empty availability `allOf` group
3. does not warn for valid availability expression
4. does not warn when tool has no availability
5. does not warn for non-expression availability metadata
6. preserves availability on the captured descriptor

## Risk / scope

- User-visible behavior change: No — descriptors are a developer contract, not user-facing runtime behavior
- Config/environment/migration: No
- Security/auth/network: No
- Highest risk: `console.warn` fires once per captured descriptor at registration time. Descriptors are cached after first capture, so the warning fires exactly once per unique tool per cache lifetime.
- Mitigation: `unsupported-signal` only fires for malformed descriptors (empty allOf/anyOf, unknown expression shapes). Runtime conditions like auth-missing are explicitly excluded.

## What did NOT change (scope boundary)

- `evaluateToolAvailability` / `evaluateExpression` — still pure, no side effects
- `buildToolPlan` — still a deterministic planner, no callbacks
- `AnyAgentTool` type — no new contract field; reading through narrowed record (same pattern as existing `label` read)
- Plugin SDK exports — no public API surface change

target.title: feat: fire session reset hooks for daily and idle resets
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Internal hooks and `before_reset` plugin hook only fire for manual `/new` and `/reset` commands, not for daily (4AM) or idle-timeout session resets. This means `session-memory` never saves on daily/idle resets.
- Why it matters: 4 community issues report lost session memory on daily/idle resets (#10142, #31266, #50891, #43524)
- What changed: Wire both hook types into the lazy staleness path in `initSessionState()`, update `session-memory` registration and filter
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): No new hook event names, no changes to plugin SDK API, no extraction/refactor — only `export` added to existing functions

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Memory / storage

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #10142
- Closes #31266
- Closes #50891
- Closes #43524
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: `initSessionState()` fires `session_end`/`session_start` plugin hooks for lazy resets but never fires `triggerInternalHook` or `hookRunner.runBeforeReset`. These only fire via `emitResetCommandHooks()` in `commands-core.ts`, which is only called for manual `/new` and `/reset` commands.
- Missing detection / guardrail: No test asserted that internal hooks fire for lazy resets
- Contributing context (if known): The lazy evaluation design means sessions aren't proactively killed — staleness is checked on next message arrival, a code path separate from manual commands

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
- Target test or file: `src/auto-reply/reply/session.stale-hooks.test.ts` (new), `src/hooks/bundled/session-memory/handler.test.ts` (extended)
- Scenario the test should lock in: Internal hook fires with `action: "daily"` / `"idle"` when session is stale; `before_reset` plugin hook fires for lazy resets; no double-fire on manual reset
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: Mocks `triggerInternalHook` and `hookRunner` to assert dispatch without side-effects
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): `session-hooks-context.test.ts` covers `session_end`/`session_start` but not internal hooks or `before_reset`

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

`session-memory` now saves session summaries on daily (4AM) and idle-timeout resets, not just manual `/new` and `/reset`. Plugin `before_reset` hook now fires for lazy resets, giving plugins pre-archive transcript access.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[lazy reset] -> session_end + session_start hooks only

After:
[lazy reset] -> internal hook (command:daily/idle) + before_reset + session_end + session_start
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Any
- Runtime/container: Node.js 24
- Integration/channel (if any): Any channel with daily or idle reset configured

### Steps

1. Configure a session with daily reset (default 4AM)
2. Have an active session with messages before 4AM
3. Send a message after 4AM (triggering lazy staleness detection)

### Expected

- `session-memory` saves a summary of the previous session
- `before_reset` plugin hook fires with `reason: "daily"` and transcript messages

### Actual (before this PR)

- `session-memory` does not fire — no summary saved
- `before_reset` plugin hook does not fire

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: Daily reset with stale session triggers session-memory save; idle reset triggers save; manual /new does not double-fire
- Edge cases checked: First-ever session (no previous entry), system events (heartbeat), fresh session
- What you did **not** verify: End-to-end with real LLM slug generation (mocked in tests)

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: `before_reset` receives the post-archive `sessionFile` path (after `renameSync`), not the original path. `loadBeforeResetTranscript` handles ENOENT by scanning for the latest `.reset.*` archived sibling.
  - Mitigation: This matches the manual reset path behavior. The stable post-archive path is intentional — it avoids racing transcript reads against the archive rename.

## AI Disclosure

- [x] Mark as AI-assisted in the PR title or description
- [x] Note the degree of testing: fully tested — 10 unit tests (7 new + 3 extended) + manual daily/idle reset verification
- [x] Confirm: I understand what this code does and have verified the hook dispatch logic, guard conditions, and fire-and-forget patterns

target.title: fix #94166: memory-core OpenAI-compatible embeddings: honor provider request.allowPrivateNetwork
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** `memory-core` OpenAI-compatible embedding requests did not carry the selected configured provider's `request.allowPrivateNetwork` transport policy into the embedding guarded-fetch policy, so memory embeddings could not honor provider-level private-network trust consistently.
- **Solution:** Reuse the existing provider trust contract at the embedding SSRF policy owner: omitted `request.allowPrivateNetwork` preserves exact-host compatibility, explicit `true` overlays broader private-network trust, and explicit `false` opts out of exact-host private-network trust.
- **What changed:** `ConfiguredEmbeddingProvider` reads `request.allowPrivateNetwork`; OpenAI-compatible memory embeddings return no exact-host SSRF policy for explicit provider denial, keep `allowedHostnames` for omitted config, and add `allowPrivateNetwork: true` only for explicit provider opt-in. Regression coverage now locks all three states.
- **What did NOT change:** Other embedding providers, non-memory model-provider request paths, gateway/channel/session routing, global SSRF helpers, schema/config surface, provider request field names, migrations, and metadata/link-local default protections are unchanged.
- **Why it matters / User impact:** Existing configured OpenAI-compatible memory provider aliases keep working by default, operators who intentionally need broader private-network trust can use the existing provider `request.allowPrivateNetwork` knob, and operators who explicitly set `request.allowPrivateNetwork: false` get the same denial semantics as the sibling model-provider transport path.
- Fixes #94166

## Origin / follow-up

N/A — independent root cause.

## Competition / linked PR analysis

- **Linked PR(s):** #94180 (`feat(memory-core): allow private network endpoints for memory embeddings`). The public-contract helper also flagged #92307 through an environment/version term, but #92307 is about exec-approval startup warnings and is not the same embedding/private-network contract.
- **Gap in linked PR(s):** #94180 adds a new memory-specific config surface, `memorySearch.remote.network.dangerouslyAllowPrivateNetwork`, for a trust decision that the selected model provider can already express with `models.providers.<id>.request.allowPrivateNetwork`.
- **Why this patch is better/canonical:** This patch reuses the existing provider transport-trust contract as the canonical source of truth for configured providers instead of introducing a second memory-only knob, while preserving shipped exact-host upgrade behavior for omitted config and honoring explicit provider denial.
- **Local real behavior proof advantage:** The local proof exercises the real `memory status --deep --json` CLI path against a loopback/private OpenAI-compatible endpoint in three states: omitted policy succeeds through exact-host trust with one `/v1/embeddings` request, explicit `request.allowPrivateNetwork: false` is blocked before the endpoint receives any request, and explicit `true` succeeds with one `/v1/embeddings` request.
- **Related open PR scan:** Open PR searches for `request.allowPrivateNetwork`, `memory-core allowPrivateNetwork`, and OpenAI-compatible private embedding endpoints found #94180 as the same-contract alternative; no broader canonical provider-inheritance PR was found besides the current PR.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** `openclaw memory status --deep` against a configured OpenAI-compatible embedding endpoint on a loopback/private address must preserve exact-host compatibility when provider private-network policy is omitted, must block when the selected provider explicitly sets `request.allowPrivateNetwork: false`, and must honor `request.allowPrivateNetwork: true` for explicit broader private-network trust.
- **Real environment tested:** Live CLI proof was captured in the local PR worktree on Linux, using the real source CLI, the real `memory-core` CLI command, isolated `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`, isolated `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, isolated `OPENCLAW_HOME`, and a local OpenAI-compatible `/v1/embeddings` HTTP endpoint bound to a loopback/private address with host and port redacted below.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** The same command was run three times with the same endpoint and separate isolated configs: omitted `models.providers.private-embedding-proof.request.allowPrivateNetwork`, explicit `request.allowPrivateNetwork: false`, and explicit `request.allowPrivateNetwork: true`.

```sh
node scripts/run-node.mjs memory status --agent proof --deep --json
```

- **Evidence after fix:** Exact-host compatibility result from the real CLI, with provider `request.allowPrivateNetwork` omitted:

```json
{
  "agentId": "proof",
  "status": {
    "provider": "openai-compatible",
    "requestedProvider": "private-embedding-proof",
    "model": "text-embedding-3-small"
  },
  "embeddingProbe": {
    "ok": true
  }
}
```

Exact-host compatibility summary:

```json
{
  "code": 0,
  "containsEmbeddingsReady": true,
  "serverRequestCount": 1
}
```

Explicit provider denial result from the real CLI:

```json
{
  "agentId": "proof",
  "status": {
    "provider": "openai-compatible",
    "requestedProvider": "private-embedding-proof",
    "model": "text-embedding-3-small"
  },
  "embeddingProbe": {
    "ok": false,
    "error": "Blocked hostname or private/internal/special-use IP address"
  }
}
```

Explicit provider denial summary:

```json
{
  "code": 0,
  "containsGuardBlock": true,
  "containsEmbeddingsReady": false,
  "serverRequestCount": 0
}
```

Provider opt-in overlay result from the real CLI:

```json
{
  "agentId": "proof",
  "status": {
    "provider": "openai-compatible",
    "requestedProvider": "private-embedding-proof",
    "model": "text-embedding-3-small"
  },
  "embeddingProbe": {
    "ok": true
  }
}
```

Provider opt-in overlay summary:

```json
{
  "code": 0,
  "containsEmbeddingsReady": true,
  "serverRequestCount": 1
}
```

Redacted endpoint request observed once in the omitted case and once in the opt-in case; the explicit false case observed zero endpoint requests:

```json
{
  "method": "POST",
  "url": "/v1/embeddings",
  "headers": {
    "host": "<loopback>:<redacted-port>",
    "authorization": "<redacted>",
    "content-type": "application/json",
    "x-proof": "openclaw-pr-94344"
  },
  "body": {
    "model": "text-embedding-3-small",
    "input": ["ping"],
    "dimensions": 3
  }
}
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The omitted configured provider alias reaches the loopback/private embedding endpoint successfully. The explicit false config is blocked by the guarded-fetch path before the endpoint sees a request. The explicit true config returns `embeddingProbe.ok: true` and reaches the endpoint once.
- **What was not tested:** I did not use a maintainer's real private LAN/VPN embedding service, third-party credentials, Mantis, Telegram, or Desktop proof infrastructure. This PR does not touch Telegram/Desktop/channel delivery; the local proof exercises the relevant memory CLI, configured-provider, and guarded-fetch runtime surface. I did not drive a live metadata/link-local endpoint for safety; metadata/link-local rebinding remains covered by the regression test that resolves an exact-host policy to `169.254.169.254` and expects private/internal blocking when broader opt-in is not set. I did not run `openclaw memory index --force`; `memory status --deep` is the requested memory runtime probe path and reaches the embedding availability check directly.
- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix

## Review findings addressed

| Finding | Status | Resolution |
| --- | --- | --- |
| RF-001 | Addressed | This update is the author-side response to the current `status: ⏳ waiting on author` label. |
| RF-002 | Fixed/proven | Explicit `models.providers.<id>.request.allowPrivateNetwork: false` now returns no embedding exact-host SSRF policy; the live CLI proof shows `embeddingProbe.ok: false`, the private/internal block message, and zero endpoint requests. |
| RF-003 | Disclosed | Memory embeddings intentionally inherit provider-level private-network trust for explicit `true`; maintainer/security acceptance remains required because this is an SSRF trust-boundary policy. |
| RF-004 | Fixed/disclosed | The focused repair aligns explicit false semantics and tests; maintainer/security review remains the final gate for the provider trust inheritance decision. |
| RF-005 | Fixed/proven | `src/plugins/openai-compatible-embedding-provider.ts` now honors explicit provider denial before constructing exact-host trust, and the owner-module regression test locks that behavior. |
| RF-006 | Fixed/proven | `CI=true OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=2 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts src/plugins/openai-compatible-embedding-provider.test.ts --reporter=verbose` passed locally with 29/29 tests. |
| RF-007 | Fixed/proven | `CI=true OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=2 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway-server.config.ts src/gateway/embeddings-http.test.ts --reporter=verbose` passed locally with 14/14 tests. |

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target test files:** `src/plugins/openai-compatible-embedding-provider.test.ts`; `src/gateway/embeddings-http.test.ts`
- **Scenario locked in:** Configured provider with `request.allowPrivateNetwork: true` sets `client.ssrfPolicy.allowedHostnames === ["llm.internal"]` and `client.ssrfPolicy.allowPrivateNetwork === true`; omitted config preserves exact-host trust and keeps `allowPrivateNetwork` unset; explicit `request.allowPrivateNetwork: false` returns no embedding SSRF policy and private/internal resolution remains blocked; configured local/private provider aliases work under omitted config; the gateway configured-provider route still reaches the local generic embedding provider.
- **Commands run after this patch:**

```sh
CI=true OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=2 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts src/plugins/openai-compatible-embedding-provider.test.ts --reporter=verbose
CI=true OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=2 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway-server.config.ts src/gateway/embeddings-http.test.ts --reporter=verbose
```

- **Observed local result:** `src/plugins/openai-compatible-embedding-provider.test.ts` passed 29/29 tests; `src/gateway/embeddings-http.test.ts` passed 14/14 tests in the local PR worktree after the explicit false repair.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** The unit tests pin the owner module's SSRF policy construction for false/omitted/true, the gateway test pins the configured provider HTTP embedding route, and the live CLI proof pins the runtime memory-core guarded-fetch path. Together they cover the exact source-of-truth boundary without adding a broader fixture or changing unrelated provider paths.

## Merge risk

- **Risk labels considered:** security-boundary, auth-provider, compatibility, session-state.
- **Risk explanation:** This touches the security boundary for private-network embedding fetches and the provider/config contract that decides when exact-host or broader private-network trust is allowed. A wrong change could relax SSRF blocking by default, break existing configured local/private aliases, or create a second incompatible memory-only trust surface.
- **Why acceptable:** The diff is limited to the OpenAI-compatible embedding provider and its tests; shipped exact-host trust for omitted configured aliases is preserved; explicit false now denies exact-host private trust; metadata/link-local rebinding remains blocked without broader opt-in; broader private-network access stays behind explicit provider-scoped opt-in; no schema, migration, or new public config knob is introduced.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** The provider `request.allowPrivateNetwork` contract is the existing source-of-truth for configured provider private-network transport trust, but the memory OpenAI-compatible embedding adapter previously built its SSRF policy from the embedding base URL without consuming the full configured provider request state. The first PR revision handled explicit `true`, but explicit `false` still fell through to exact-host trust.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** The trust decision now lives where the embedding SSRF policy is created: explicit provider denial returns no exact-host policy, omitted config preserves the existing exact-host policy, and explicit provider opt-in overlays broader `allowPrivateNetwork` trust. This fixes source policy construction instead of masking blocked fetches downstream or adding a parallel memory-only override.
- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High — the patch stays focused on the embedding provider policy and configured-provider embedding test coverage, includes real CLI proof for the runtime path, preserves existing alias compatibility, handles explicit denial, and leaves the final product/security acceptance explicitly called out for maintainer review.
- **Patch quality notes:** Patch-quality warnings are expected and reviewed: the `unknown as EmbeddingProviderCreateOptions["config"]` casts are test fixture casts for partial config objects, not production type suppression; returning `undefined` for explicit false is the intentional denial path and matches the sibling model-provider behavior where no exact-origin trust is attached. No skipped tests, `.only`, lint disables, or runtime broad catch were added.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** The canonical contract surface is `models.providers.<id>.request.allowPrivateNetwork` for provider transport trust. This patch makes memory embedding SSRF policy consume that source-of-truth contract before guarded fetch runs; it does not change schema/default behavior, does not add migration or partial override semantics, and intentionally leaves other providers and non-memory request paths out of scope.

target.title: fix(ios): stabilize chat streaming layout and session flow
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: iOS chat streaming could attach to a synthetic placeholder lane, churn during initial history/bootstrap, and reparse streaming content in ways that caused flicker or session drift.
- Why it matters: active conversations can show unstable streaming UI, lose the correct lane/session association, or visibly flicker while tokens arrive.
- What changed: scoped streaming to the active session key, hardened stream acceptance/initial load behavior, and stabilized layout parsing/visibility updates.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no gateway protocol changes, no new model behavior. This fix lives in the shared `OpenClawChatUI` layer used by both iOS and macOS. The bug (cross-run UI drift during concurrent session-key agent events) affects any client using `ChatViewModel`; macOS uses the same chat view and benefits equally, but the fix is not platform-specific — it guards shared streaming state.
- AI-assisted: yes (AI-assisted implementation, contributor-reviewed).
- Testing level: targeted shared-package tests plus iOS build validation.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #40985
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: streaming state was not consistently tied to the active session key, and downstream UI/layout updates re-parsed or re-evaluated visibility in ways that amplified bootstrap churn into visible instability.
- Missing detection / guardrail: there was insufficient coverage around session-scoped streaming updates plus layout stability under rapid token updates/history refresh.
- Prior context (`git blame`, prior PR, issue, or refactor if known): this branch supersedes #40985 by fixing the broader lane/acceptance/layout causes instead of only the earlier visible symptom.
- Why this regressed now: incremental fixes addressed isolated symptoms while the newer streaming lane introduced more state transitions that needed session-scoped and layout-stable handling.
- If unknown, what was ruled out: the issue was not isolated to one text-rendering widget; it involved session scoping, bootstrap timing, and layout parsing together.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `apps/shared/OpenClawKit/Tests/OpenClawKitTests/ChatViewModelTests.swift`
  - `apps/shared/OpenClawKit/Tests/OpenClawKitTests/AssistantTextParserTests.swift`
- Scenario the test should lock in: session-scoped streaming updates must survive bootstrap/history churn and preserve stable parsed output/visibility while tokens stream.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug lives in shared chat state/parsing logic, so package-level tests cover the core behavior without requiring a full end-to-end harness.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): targeted parser/view-model tests were added/expanded for this branch.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- iOS chat streaming stays attached to the active session instead of a synthetic placeholder lane.
- Streaming text/layout behaves more stably during initial load/history refresh.
- Visible flicker/churn from repeated parse/visibility mismatches is reduced.

## Diagram (if applicable)

N/A

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`): No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`): No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS local dev environment
- Runtime/container: shared OpenClawKit package + iOS app build
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): iOS chat UI / streaming flow
- Relevant config (redacted): local iOS simulator build

### Steps

1. Build the shared package and iOS app from this branch.
2. Open a chat session and trigger a streaming reply.
3. Observe initial history/bootstrap plus active token streaming.

### Expected

- Streaming binds to the active session.
- Bootstrap/history churn does not destabilize the active stream.
- Layout remains visually stable while tokens arrive.

### Actual

- Matched expected in targeted tests and local build validation.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios:
  - `swift test --package-path apps/shared/OpenClawKit --filter ChatViewModelTests`
  - `swift test --package-path apps/shared/OpenClawKit --filter AssistantTextParserTests`
  - `xcodegen generate`
  - `xcodebuild -project apps/ios/OpenClaw.xcodeproj -scheme OpenClaw -destination 'generic/platform=iOS Simulator' CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO build`
- Edge cases checked:
  - stream acceptance during bootstrap/history load
  - parser stability under repeated streaming updates
- What you did **not** verify:
  - full repository CI matrix
  - non-iOS clients

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
  - All 19 review threads (P1/P2 from `chatgpt-codex-connector[bot]` + `copilot-pull-request-reviewer`) resolved in this update.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`): Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`): No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A



## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: chat-state timing bugs can reappear under bootstrapping or external-final sync edge cases.
  - Mitigation: this patch scopes state to the active session and adds targeted parser/view-model coverage around the churn paths.



## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: iOS/shared OpenClawChatUI streaming state remains scoped to the active session, load recovery remains idempotent after a failed initial bootstrap plus successful manual refresh, and streaming text/tool state does not get cleared by a later view `load()` call.
- **Real environment tested**: macOS local dev environment, Xcode 26.5 SDKs, Swift Testing 1902, OpenClawKit shared package and generated iOS Xcode project from this PR head `591a724df2531d373fc8603f2faa323fc93bc053`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```bash
swift test --package-path apps/shared/OpenClawKit --filter ChatViewModelTests
swift test --package-path apps/shared/OpenClawKit --filter AssistantTextParserTests
xcodegen generate --spec apps/ios/project.yml
xcodebuild -project apps/ios/OpenClaw.xcodeproj -scheme OpenClawChatUI -destination 'generic/platform=iOS Simulator' CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO build
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

```text
ChatViewModelTests: Test run with 83 tests in 1 suite passed after 5.550 seconds.
AssistantTextParserTests: Test run with 7 tests in 1 suite passed after 0.001 seconds.
xcodegen generate --spec apps/ios/project.yml: Created project at /Users/eulices/projects/openclaw-pr50483/apps/ios/OpenClaw.xcodeproj
xcodebuild OpenClawChatUI generic iOS Simulator: ** BUILD SUCCEEDED **
```

- **Observed result after fix**: The recovered-load regression is covered by `successful refresh after failed load makes later load idempotent`, which passed after confirming a failed first bootstrap can recover through manual refresh and a later `load()` does not issue another history request or clear the active stream. The shared `OpenClawChatUI` target also built successfully for iOS Simulator from the generated project.
- **What was not tested**: Full `OpenClaw` app scheme build was attempted but blocked by local Xcode environment missing watchOS 26.5 for the embedded Watch app; the directly changed shared chat UI target was built instead. Full repository CI matrix was left to GitHub.
